meaning: adj.宸插垎瑙ｇ殑,宸茶厫鐑傜殑
examples: Water can be resolved [decomposed] into hydrogen and oxygen.
In the best performance, color removal was 92.1%, and basic rose red was decomposed completely.
A task cannot be further decomposed.
Her remains were found in Washington's Rock Creek Park more than a year later, badly decomposed.
The land combines limestone, clay-limestone, slate and, further west from Mr. Gauby's estate, decomposed granite.
